```{r setup, echo = F}
#knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = T, message = FALSE, warning = FALSE, error = FALSE)
knitr::opts_chunk$set(warning = FALSE, message = FALSE, dpi = 40, fig.retina = 1, dev = "svglite", jpeg.quality = 85 )
#warning suppression
storeWarn<- getOption("warn")
options(warn = -1)
options(bitmapType='cairo')
```

```{r packages, echo=F, error=F, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE}
library(ggVennDiagram) 
library(tidyverse)
library(pheatmap)
#library(data.table)
library(matrixStats)
library(ggplot2)
library(DT)
library(PCAtools)
library(RColorBrewer)
library(readxl)
library(limma)
library(glue)
library(xlsx)
library(magick) # to rasterize plots to make it smaller
```

# Analysis of miRNA array data from patients with Fuchs' dystrophy

Тут общие слова, что зачем цель, задавчи, сколько образцов etc.\
перенести р сетап + чанк с лайбрари, емл дописать! экспортитровать таблицу с Norm data, сделать ПСА.

1 подружить мета и данные по названию 2 QC дистрибьюшн, PCA

# Importing data from nSolver

The data was normalized and processed in nSolver (Nanostring), and then analyzed manually. Positive control samples, ligation samples, and other samples must be removed from the table.

```{r data_import, eval=T, echo = F}
#extract data as numeric
norm_data <- read.delim('/data7a/bio/human_genomics/fuchs_dystrophy/nanostring/analisys/upload/lig_norm_data.tsv', header = T, dec = ",", skip = 2)
colnames(norm_data) <- read.delim('/data7a/bio/human_genomics/fuchs_dystrophy/nanostring/analisys/upload/lig_norm_data.tsv', header = F, nrow = 1)

norm_data <- norm_data[norm_data$`Class Name`=="Endogenous1",]
## probes metadata
other_data <- norm_data[, 1:19]
norm_data <- norm_data[,20:ncol(norm_data)]

row.names(norm_data) <- other_data$`Probe Name`

saveRDS(norm_data, file="/data7a/bio/human_genomics/fuchs_dystrophy/nanostring/analisys/RDS/norm_data.RDS")
```

import other normalizations

```{r raw_import, echo = F}
# raw data
#extract data as numeric
raw_data <- read.delim('/data7a/bio/human_genomics/fuchs_dystrophy/nanostring/analisys/upload/raw_data.tsv', header = T, dec = ",", skip = 2)
colnames(raw_data) <- read.delim('/data7a/bio/human_genomics/fuchs_dystrophy/nanostring/analisys/upload/raw_data.tsv', header = F, nrow = 1)

raw_data <- raw_data[raw_data$`Class Name`=="Endogenous1",]
## probes metadata
other_data1 <- raw_data[, 1:19]
raw_data <- raw_data[,20:ncol(raw_data)]

row.names(raw_data) <- other_data1$`Probe Name`


#hkg normalisation
hkg_norm_data <- read.delim('/data7a/bio/human_genomics/fuchs_dystrophy/nanostring/analisys/upload/hkg_norm_data.tsv', header = T, dec = ",", skip = 2)
colnames(hkg_norm_data) <- read.delim('/data7a/bio/human_genomics/fuchs_dystrophy/nanostring/analisys/upload/hkg_norm_data.tsv', header = F, nrow = 1)

hkg_norm_data <- hkg_norm_data[hkg_norm_data$`Class Name`=="Endogenous1",]
## probes metadata
other_data2 <- hkg_norm_data[, 1:19]
hkg_norm_data <- hkg_norm_data[,20:ncol(hkg_norm_data)]
row.names(hkg_norm_data) <- other_data2$`Probe Name`


#ligation normalisation (recommended)
lig_norm_data <- read.delim('/data7a/bio/human_genomics/fuchs_dystrophy/nanostring/analisys/upload/lig_norm_data.tsv', header = T, dec = ",", skip = 2)
colnames(lig_norm_data) <- read.delim('/data7a/bio/human_genomics/fuchs_dystrophy/nanostring/analisys/upload/lig_norm_data.tsv', header = F, nrow = 1)

lig_norm_data <- lig_norm_data[lig_norm_data$`Class Name`=="Endogenous1",]
## probes metadata
other_data3 <- lig_norm_data[, 1:19]
lig_norm_data <- lig_norm_data[,20:ncol(lig_norm_data)]
row.names(lig_norm_data) <- other_data3$`Probe Name`


#allgenes normalisation
all_norm_data <- read.delim('/data7a/bio/human_genomics/fuchs_dystrophy/nanostring/analisys/upload/all_norm_data.tsv', header = T, dec = ",", skip = 2)
colnames(all_norm_data) <- read.delim('/data7a/bio/human_genomics/fuchs_dystrophy/nanostring/analisys/upload/all_norm_data.tsv', header = F, nrow = 1)

all_norm_data <- all_norm_data[all_norm_data$`Class Name`=="Endogenous1",]
## probes metadata
other_data3 <- all_norm_data[, 1:19]
all_norm_data <- all_norm_data[,20:ncol(all_norm_data)]
row.names(all_norm_data) <- other_data3$`Probe Name`


#lig normalisation wo positive probes, with background tresholding
trh_lig_norm_data <- read.delim('/data7a/bio/human_genomics/fuchs_dystrophy/nanostring/analisys/upload/lig_wo_posk_with_bgthresh.tsv', header = T, dec = ",", skip = 2)
colnames(trh_lig_norm_data) <- read.delim('/data7a/bio/human_genomics/fuchs_dystrophy/nanostring/analisys/upload/lig_wo_posk_with_bgthresh.tsv', header = F, nrow = 1)
trh_lig_norm_data <- trh_lig_norm_data[trh_lig_norm_data$`Class Name`=="Endogenous1",]
## probes metadata
other_data3 <- trh_lig_norm_data[, 1:19]
trh_lig_norm_data <- trh_lig_norm_data[,20:ncol(trh_lig_norm_data)]
row.names(trh_lig_norm_data) <- other_data3$`Probe Name`


#lig normalisation wo positive probes, wo background tresholding
nopos_lig_norm_data <- read.delim('/data7a/bio/human_genomics/fuchs_dystrophy/nanostring/analisys/upload/lig_wo_posk_wo_bgthresh.tsv', header = T, dec = ",", skip = 2)
colnames(nopos_lig_norm_data) <- read.delim('/data7a/bio/human_genomics/fuchs_dystrophy/nanostring/analisys/upload/lig_wo_posk_wo_bgthresh.tsv', header = F, nrow = 1)
nopos_lig_norm_data <- nopos_lig_norm_data[nopos_lig_norm_data$`Class Name`=="Endogenous1",]
## probes metadata
other_data3 <- nopos_lig_norm_data[, 1:19]
nopos_lig_norm_data <- nopos_lig_norm_data[,20:ncol(nopos_lig_norm_data)]
row.names(nopos_lig_norm_data) <- other_data3$`Probe Name`
```

### Do the logarithm of the expression data

Why can't we use raw data for PCA right away? For PCA, we need logarithmically smoothed data; outliers in the data will greatly distort the picture. Expression differences can vary by a factor of a million. We need to reduce the absolute difference between features; PCA works with absolute variances, so they need to be strictly logarithmized. Conventionally, we need to narrow the broad scale.

If the difference is three orders of magnitude, then 10 log; if less than 2 log. But if the range is small, then, conversely, we take the square root. (The larger the logarithm base, the smaller the range the data will be in)

What order of difference? (6 - 10,000, for example) =\> log10.

```{r log_change}
log_data <- lapply(norm_data, log10)
log_data <- as.data.frame(log_data)
rownames(log_data) <-  other_data$`Probe Name`
```

### Importing and adjusting metadata

We read our objects with metadata and mark comparison groups with colors.

> Pheatmap and PCATools require that the rownames (samples) in the metadata table match the colnames (samples) in the expression tables.

**Добавим новую метадату с поправленным полом.**

```{r metadata}
metadata <- read_delim("/data7a/bio/human_genomics/fuchs_dystrophy/nanostring/analisys/download/metadata_with_sex.csv", 
delim = ";", escape_double = FALSE, trim_ws = TRUE)

metadata <- metadata[order(metadata$Sample_name), ] # возвращает list
metadata <- as.data.frame(metadata)
metadata$Sample_name <- gsub('_endo', '', metadata$Sample_name)

metadata$run <- factor(metadata$run, levels = c(1, 2, 3))
metadata <- metadata %>%  mutate(Group = if_else(FECD == "yes", "FECD", "Control"))
metadata$Group <- factor(metadata$Group)


rownames(metadata) <- metadata$Sample_name # for PCA
#datatable(metadata, rownames=TRUE, caption = "FECD metadata")
meta4limma <- metadata
metadata <- metadata %>% dplyr::rename('Sex' = 'sex', 'Age' = 'age', 'Run' ='run')
saveRDS(metadata, file="/data7a/bio/human_genomics/fuchs_dystrophy/nanostring/analisys/RDS/metadata.RDS")
```

# PCA {.tabset}

We're setting up a PCA object, leaving the variability cutoff at 10% for now to remove noise. We had to downgrade [matrixStats](https://github.com/satijalab/seurat/issues/7501#issuecomment-1854571904).

Let's build a PCA without the bad samples that we discarded in the downstream analysis.

```{r pca_set}
# need this
options(matrixStats.useNames.NA = "deprecated")
# remotes::install_version("matrixStats", version="1.1.0") # restart your session and run previous scripts

metadata_pca <- metadata[!(metadata$Sample_name %in% c('Dfu_209','Dfu_210', 'Dfu_223', 'C_209', 'C_210', 'C_211')), ]
log_data_pca <- log_data[, colnames(log_data) %in% metadata_pca$Sample_name]

pca <- pca(log_data_pca, metadata = metadata_pca, removeVar = 0.1)
rm(log_data_pca)
```

## Screeplot

This plot helps us understand up to what component it makes sense to evaluate variability. The component numbers indicate the order of importance of each component in explaining data variance. The first component explains \~80% of the data. This means that the data are highly contrasting, those with high variability, and so on.

```{r pca_screeplot}
screeplot(pca, components = getComponents(pca, seq_len(6)))
```

## Pairsplot

This plot shows all possible component combinations for assessing which component divides groups according to different principles.

I limited the number of components to four, since one component is still the main one.

```{r pca_pairsplot}
pairsplot(pca, components = getComponents(pca, seq_len(4)),
          colby = 'fecd_exp',
          triangle = TRUE, trianglelabSize = 6, titleLabSize = 6, axisLabSize=5, legendPosition = 'none')
```

## Loadings

This graph allows us to understand which genes determine which component. That is, if the expected groups are well separated by a component, we can immediately determine which genes contribute most, and in which direction the component deviates based on sign.

Conclusion from the plot: we look at which miRNAs are in the component, with a cutoff for low contribution. On PC1, all the miRNAs are clustered together.

```{r pca_loadings}
plotloadings(pca, components = getComponents(pca, seq_len(2)), rangeRetain = 0.03,
  col = c("red", "white", "blue"), colMidpoint = 0,
  shape = 21, shapeSizeRange = c(3, 3),
  axisLabSize = 10,  titleLabSize = 10)
```

## Eigencorrplot

This graph allows us to understand which metadata parameters are correlated with which principal components. This means, when combined with previous data, we can understand which genes determine variability in which metadata component.

The Y-axis shows the metadata pulled from our_meta. Stars indicate the correlation significance; the more stars there are, the higher the correlation.

```{r pca_eigencorrplot, eval = T}
eigencorplot(pca, components = getComponents(pca, seq_len(4)), 
             metavars = c("Expaned_alleles", "fecd_exp", "exp", "Age"))
```

## Biplot {.tabset}

Using a biplot, we can visualize the dispersion of samples in the space of specific components.

Now let's look at biplots---graphs in the space of two principal components. In biplots, you can change the component numbers for visualization.

### 1-2 group

We look at the clustering to see if the components are separated by a straight line. If not, other components are at play.

```{r biplot_12233, echo = F, fig.height=8, fig.width=9}
xpca <- 1
ypca <- 2
col_by <-"Group"

biplot(pca, x = "PC1", y = "PC2", pointSize = 7,
       colby = col_by, 
       legendPosition="right", showLoadings = F,
       title = glue("Principle components on miRNA assay", ": ", col_by))
```

1.  The graph depicts the distribution of diseased and healthy samples in a two-component space.
2.  It is clear that the two groups of samples:
    1.  They are visually separable and do not mix.

    2.  They cluster well within their groups.

    3.  There is a small cluster of controls in the middle of the graph.
3.  The data are suitable for FECD vs. Control analysis. The outlier in the center of the graph is likely of an unknown biological origin.

### 1-2 expansion

```{r echo = F, message = F, warning = F, error = F, fig.height=8, fig.width=9}
xpca <- 1
ypca <- 2
col_by <-"fecd_exp"

biplot(pca, x = "PC1", y = "PC2", pointSize = 7, colby = col_by, 
       legendPosition = "right", showLoadings = F,
       title = glue("Principle components on miRNA assay", ": ", col_by))
```

To compare the heterogeneity of the comparison groups, a principal component analysis was performed. The plot of the first two components shows that dystrophic and control samples cluster, with the presence or absence of expansion inseparable. Thus, the endothelial microRNA profile obtained using Nanostring technology is associated with the presence of Fuchs' corneal dystrophy.

### 1-2 sex difference plot

```{r echo = F, message = F, warning = F, error = F, fig.height=8, fig.width=9}
xpca <- 1
ypca <- 2
col_by <- "Sex"
#sex_difference echo = F, message = F, warning = F, error = F, fig.height=8, fig.width=9

biplot_run <- biplot(pca, x = "PC1", y = "PC2", pointSize = 7, colby = col_by, 
       shape = "Group", 
       shapekey = c(FECD=18, Control=17),
       legendPosition = "right", showLoadings = F,
       title = glue("Principle components on miRNA assay", ": ", col_by))

biplot_run
```

### 1-2 run difference

```{r echo = F, message = F, warning = F, error = F, fig.height=8, fig.width=9}
xpca <- 1
ypca <- 2
col_by <-'Run'

biplot_run <- biplot(pca, x = "PC1", y = "PC2", pointSize = 6.5, colby = col_by, 
                    
       shape = "Group", labSize=3,
       shapekey = c(FECD=16, Control=17), encircleLineSize =100,
       legendPosition = "right", showLoadings = FALSE, drawConnectors = T,
       min.segment.length = 0,
       title = glue("Principle components on miRNA assay", ": ", col_by))
biplot_run
```

1.  The graph shows the instrument run number associated with the samples.
2.  It is clear that most patients were sequenced on one chip, while most controls were sequenced on the other two.
3.  A batch effect is possible when comparing expression patterns for patients and healthy controls, as the data were obtained from different instrument runs.

### 1-2 age difference

```{r echo = F, message = F, warning = F, error = F, fig.height=8, fig.width=9}
xpca <- 1
ypca <- 2
col_by <- "Age"

biplot(pca, x = "PC1", y = "PC2", pointSize = 7, colby = col_by, 
       shape = "Group", 
       shapekey = c(FECD=18, Control=17),
       legendPosition = "right", showLoadings = F,
        title = glue("Principle components on miRNA assay", ": ", col_by )
       ) + scale_color_gradientn(
    colors = c("#E6F2FF", "#6BAED6", "#084594") )
rm(pca)
```

1.  We will evaluate the age grouping of samples in the diseased and healthy groups using the two principal component space.
2.  The results show that the collected samples are not balanced by age. The FECD group is significantly older than the control group.
3.  For a more reliable biological result, controls should have been selected from a closer age group. The average age difference between a diseased and healthy individual in the sample is \~20 years.

# Heatmap {.tabset}

Let's compare hitmaps of different normalizations from nSolver.

Reason: The raw data contains counts of the form 0, 1, 2, 3. Top 100 normalization significantly increases the values ​​in the Dfu samples. Ultimately, based on the hitmap results, we switched to ligation normalization.

## raw_data

```{r, fig.height=15, fig.width=13}
heatmap(as.matrix(raw_data), cexCol = 1.5)
```

## top100 normalisation

топ100 и лигация совпадают, тк мы взяли лигирование как norm_data.

```{r, fig.height=14, fig.width=13}
heatmap(as.matrix(norm_data), cexCol = 2)

```

## housekeeping genes normalisation

```{r, fig.height=15, fig.width=13}
heatmap(as.matrix(hkg_norm_data), cexCol = 1.5)
```

## ligation normalisation

```{r, fig.height=15, fig.width=13}
heatmap(as.matrix(lig_norm_data), cexCol = 1.5)
```

## Ligation w/o pos probes w/o thresholding

```{r, fig.height=15, fig.width=13}
heatmap(as.matrix(nopos_lig_norm_data), cexCol = 1.5)
```

## Ligation w/o pos probes with thresholding

```{r, fig.height=15, fig.width=13}
heatmap(as.matrix(trh_lig_norm_data), cexCol = 1.5)
```

## Allgenes normalisation

```{r, fig.height=15, fig.width=13}
heatmap(as.matrix(all_norm_data), cexCol = 1.5)
```

```{r echo=F}
#rm unused
rm(hkg_norm_data, lig_norm_data, other_data1, other_data2, other_data3, all_norm_data, trh_lig_norm_data, raw_data, nopos_lig_norm_data)
```

# Summary statistics

Let's create a table with statistics. Query the Fisher test package for the contingency table. We have 0 expanded counterfields, which interferes with the Fisher test, so we'll increase the number to 1, and correspondingly increase the number of controls without expansion, thereby maintaining the proportion, assuming that 5% of the population is expanded.

Metadata without discarded samples, without trimming for paired transcriptomes.

```{r summary_statistics_df, echo = F}

metadata <- metadata_pca
count_f <- sum(metadata$FECD == "yes")
count_c <- sum(metadata$FECD == "no")
male_f <- sum(metadata$sex[metadata$FECD == "yes"] == "male")
female_f <- sum(metadata$sex[metadata$FECD == "yes"] == "female")
male_c <- sum(metadata$sex[metadata$FECD == "no"] == "male")
female_c <- sum(metadata$sex[metadata$FECD == "no"] == "female")
# age fm
male_age <- metadata$Age[metadata$sex == "male"]
male_age <- metadata$Age[metadata$sex == "male"]
female_age <- metadata$Age[metadata$sex == "female"]
age_sd_male <- signif(sd(male_age), digits=3)
age_sd_female <- signif(sd(female_age), digits=3)

paste("mean M, F age:", mean(male_age), mean(female_age))


# sd
age_f <- metadata$Age[metadata$FECD == "yes"]
age_f_male <- metadata$Age[metadata$FECD == "yes" & metadata$sex == 'male']
age_f_female <- metadata$Age[metadata$FECD == "yes" & metadata$sex == 'female']

age_c <- metadata$Age[metadata$FECD == "no"]
age_c_male <- metadata$Age[metadata$FECD == "no" & metadata$sex == 'male']
age_c_female <- metadata$Age[metadata$FECD == "no" & metadata$sex == 'female']


paste("CONTROL mean M, F age:", mean(age_c_male), mean(age_c_female))
paste("FECD mean M, F age:", mean(age_f_male), mean(age_f_female))


paste("CONTROL SD mean M, F age:", signif(sd(age_c_male), digits=3), signif(sd(age_c_female), digits=3) )
paste("FECD SD mean M, F age:", signif(sd(age_f_male), digits=3), signif(sd(age_f_female), digits=3) )
 

age_sd_f <- signif(sd(metadata$Age[metadata$FECD == "yes"]), digits=3)
age_sd_c <- signif(sd(metadata$Age[metadata$FECD == "no"]), digits=2)
exp_f <-sum(metadata$exp[metadata$FECD == 'yes'] == 'yes')
exp_c <- sum(metadata$exp[metadata$FECD == 'no'] == 'yes')

cohort_stats <- data.frame(
  FECD = c(
    Patients = count_f,
    Males = male_f,
    `Mean age ± sd` = paste(signif(mean(age_f), digits=2), "±", age_sd_f),
    Expanded = exp_f),
  
  Control = c(
    count_c, male_c, 
    paste(signif(mean(age_c), digits=2), "±", age_sd_c), exp_c)
)

# делаем подтаблицу к каждой строке для расчётов
contingency_table_sex <- data.frame(FECD = c(Males = male_f, Females = female_f), Control = c(male_c, female_c))
contingency_table_exp <-  data.frame(Exp_yes = c(FECD = exp_f, Control = exp_c + 1), Exp_no = c(16 - exp_f, 19 + exp_c))

# fisher test, age t-test
ft_sex <- fisher.test(contingency_table_sex)
ft_exp <- fisher.test(contingency_table_exp)
ttest_age <- t.test(age_f, age_c)

cohort_stats$`OR + Pval` <- c(NA, 
                              paste('OR =', signif(ft_sex$estimate, 2), 'pval =', signif(ft_sex$p.value, 2)), 
                              paste('T =', signif(ttest_age$statistic, 2), 'pval =', signif(ttest_age$p.value, 2)), 
                              paste('OR =', signif(ft_exp$estimate, 2), 'pval =', signif(ft_exp$p.value, 2)))

#write_csv2(cohort_stats ,'/data7a/bio/human_genomics/fuchs_dystrophy/nanostring/analisys/download/meta_cohort_stats.csv')
#datatable(cohort_stats)
cohort_stats
```

# Differential expression

First, we'll calculate differential expression using a t-test and the nonparametric Mann-Whitney test. Limma is the core of edgeR, DESEQ, and other tools.

> Limma is a general-purpose package that can be used in any analysis on any data, as it doesn't have heuristics specific to specific data types and applications, like deseq for ngs. Limma builds a negative binomial distribution model and adjusts the weights of different genes to fit it. It works well with small samples, but for samples larger than 500, classic methods (t-test, U-test) can be used.

```{r rm_outliers}
# no outliers
norm_data_cleaned <- norm_data[!(colnames(norm_data) %in% c('C_209', 'C_210', 'C_211', 'Dfu_209', 'Dfu_210', 'Dfu_223'))]
meta4limma_test <- meta4limma[!(meta4limma$Sample_name %in% c('C_209', 'C_210', 'C_211', 'Dfu_209',  'Dfu_210', 'Dfu_223')), ]
```

## t-test, u-test, limma

What's the test's direction – we divide DFU by Control.

-   LFC \> 0 → up‑regulation in FECD.

-   LFC \< 0 → down‑regulation.

We need to remove the miRNA where **BOTH values ​​are low across groups**, as such pairs **are artifactual**. A value where one is 1 and the other is 0 is ok. We'll immediately filter them out of the DEGs.

```{r ttest_diff_expr}
gr_1 <- sum(grepl("C_", colnames(norm_data_cleaned)))   #8 end of 1 gr
gr_2 <- gr_1 + 1 #  2 gr start


deg_ttest <- do.call(rbind, 
                      lapply(seq(1, nrow(norm_data_cleaned)), function(x)
  {f_tt <- t.test(norm_data_cleaned[x, 1:gr_1], norm_data_cleaned[x, gr_2:ncol(norm_data_cleaned)])
  
  mean_c <- (mean(unlist(norm_data_cleaned[x, 1:gr_1])))
  mean_f <- (mean(unlist((norm_data_cleaned[x, gr_2:ncol(norm_data_cleaned)]))))
  
  fc <- log2(mean_f / mean_c)
  
  return (unlist(c(fc, f_tt$p.value, mean_f, mean_c)))
  }
))

deg_ttest <- as.data.frame(deg_ttest)
colnames(deg_ttest) <- c('lfc', 't_pval', 'mean_f', 'mean_c')
rownames(deg_ttest) <-  other_data$`Probe Name`
deg_ttest <- deg_ttest %>% rownames_to_column('miRNA')

# filter ZERO miRNA in BOTH groups
low_mirna <- deg_ttest %>% filter((mean_c < 2.2) & (mean_f < 2.2)) %>% pull(miRNA)
deg_ttest <- deg_ttest %>% filter(!(miRNA %in% low_mirna))
deg_ttest$p_adj <- p.adjust(deg_ttest$t_pval, "fdr")

# u-test
deg_utest <- do.call(rbind, 
                      lapply(seq(1, nrow(norm_data_cleaned)), function(x)
  {u_tt <- wilcox.test(as.numeric(norm_data_cleaned[x, 1:gr_1]), as.numeric(norm_data_cleaned[x, gr_2:ncol(norm_data_cleaned)]))
  fc <- log2(
    mean(unlist((norm_data_cleaned[x, gr_2:ncol(norm_data_cleaned)]))) / mean(unlist(norm_data_cleaned[x, 1:gr_1]))
    )
  return (unlist(c(fc, u_tt$p.value)))
  }
))
deg_utest <- as.data.frame(deg_utest)
colnames(deg_utest) <- c('lfc', 'u_pval')
deg_utest$p_adj <- p.adjust(deg_utest$u_pval, "fdr")
rownames(deg_utest) <-  other_data$`Probe Name`
deg_utest <- deg_utest %>% rownames_to_column('miRNA') %>% filter(!(miRNA %in% low_mirna))

#limma
# create groups based on metadata
group <- factor(meta4limma_test$FECD, levels = c("no", "yes"))
# we create a design matrix, ~0 means without including the intercept (0)   
design <- model.matrix(~0 + group)
colnames(design) <- c("Control", "FECD")
# into matrix and log
limma_matrix <- as.matrix(norm_data_cleaned)
limma_matrix <- log2(limma_matrix)

# applying a linear regression model to a matrix
limma_fit <- lmFit(limma_matrix, design)

# Contrasts for comparing groups
contrast_matrix <- makeContrasts(FECD - Control, levels = design)
fit2 <- contrasts.fit(limma_fit, contrast_matrix)
# Bayesian analysis
fit2 <- eBayes(fit2)

# retrieving results
deg_limma <- topTable(fit2, adjust.method = "fdr", number = Inf)
deg_limma <- deg_limma %>% mutate(miRNA = rownames(.)) %>% filter(!(miRNA %in% low_mirna))
rm(fit2, limma_fit, limma_matrix, design, group, meta4limma_test)
```

Let's also filter the dataframe

```{r}
saveRDS(low_mirna, file="/data7a/bio/human_genomics/fuchs_dystrophy/nanostring/analisys/RDS/low_mirna.RDS")
norm_data_cleaned <- norm_data_cleaned %>% filter(!(rownames(.) %in% low_mirna))
```

**Filter DEGs**

We filter the tables to obtain only significant microRNAs. To do this, we need to select a table consisting of rows with values ​​such that lfc \> 1, lfc \< -1, and p_adj \< 0.05.

```{r filter_alltests}
filtered_tt <- deg_ttest %>%
  filter(p_adj <= 0.05 & (lfc > 1 | lfc < -1))
filtered_ut <- deg_utest %>%
  filter(p_adj <= 0.05 & (lfc > 1 | lfc < -1))
filtered_limma <- deg_limma %>%
  filter(adj.P.Val <= 0.05 & (logFC > 1 | logFC < -1))
```

## Intersection of genes from all tests

```{r VennDiagram, fig.height=5, fig.width=5, eval=T, echo=F}
venn <- list(T_test = as.vector((filtered_tt$miRNA)), U_test = filtered_ut$miRNA, Limma = as.vector(rownames(filtered_limma)))
ggVennDiagram(venn) + scale_fill_gradient(low = "#F4FAFE", high = "#4981BF")
```

**Merge list of differential expression genes. Export.**

We'll select genes that are present in all differential expression lists. We'll retain the LFC values ​​to allow for overlap with DEGs from another article.

```{r unite_diffexpr}
diffexpressed_mirna <- Reduce(intersect, list((filtered_tt$miRNA), (filtered_ut$miRNA), rownames(filtered_limma))) 
diffexpressed_mirna <- filtered_limma[ diffexpressed_mirna, ]  
saveRDS(diffexpressed_mirna, file="/data7a/bio/human_genomics/fuchs_dystrophy/nanostring/analisys/RDS/diffexpressed_mirna_v2.RDS")
#DT::datatable(diffexpressed_mirna, filter = 'top')
diffexpressed_mirna
```

**Preprocessing for further analysis**

We need to obtain the names of differentially expressed miRNAs in the HGNC entry for further processing, without hsa, and move the information about 3/5p to a separate column. The resulting table contains differential expressions with the classic entry, the HGNC symbol, and the MIMAT ID.

```{r formatting_names_diff_mirna}
# hsa-miR-1234-3p -> MIR2114 
diffexpressed_mirna_HGNC <- sapply(diffexpressed_mirna$miRNA, toupper)
diffexpressed_mirna_HGNC <- gsub('HSA-', '', diffexpressed_mirna_HGNC)
diffexpressed_mirna_HGNC <- as.data.frame(diffexpressed_mirna_HGNC, row.names = NULL)
colnames(diffexpressed_mirna_HGNC) <- 'HGNC_symbol'
# 5/3p 
diffexpressed_mirna_HGNC$strand <- ifelse(grepl("5P", diffexpressed_mirna_HGNC$HGNC_symbol), "5P",
                             ifelse(grepl("3P", diffexpressed_mirna_HGNC$HGNC_symbol), "3P", "5P"))

diffexpressed_mirna_HGNC$HGNC_symbol <- gsub('-', '', gsub('-5P', '', gsub('-3P', '', diffexpressed_mirna_HGNC$HGNC_symbol)))

# вручную добавляем названия мирнк (почему - см ниже)
diffexpressed_mirna_HGNC <- rbind(diffexpressed_mirna_HGNC, data.frame(HGNC_symbol = c("MIR128-1", "MIR128-2", "MIR1283-1", "MIR1283-2"), strand = c('-', '-', '-', '-'), stringsAsFactors = FALSE))

diffexpressed_mirna_HGNC$miRNA <- row.names(diffexpressed_mirna_HGNC)

# MIMAT id 
diffexpressed_mirna_HGNC <- merge(diffexpressed_mirna_HGNC, other_data[, c("Probe Name", "Accession #")], 
                          by.x = "miRNA", by.y = "Probe Name")
diffexpressed_mirna_HGNC <- rename(diffexpressed_mirna_HGNC, 'MIMAT_ID' = "Accession #")

saveRDS(diffexpressed_mirna_HGNC, file="/data7a/bio/human_genomics/fuchs_dystrophy/nanostring/analisys/RDS/diffexpressed_mirna_HGNC.RDS")

#DT::datatable(diffexpressed_mirna_HGNC, caption = "Дифэкспрессированная микроРНК")
diffexpressed_mirna_HGNC
```

### Volcano plot DEG

We are building a Volcano plot using all our DEGs.

```{r mir_volcano, echo=F}
logFC_cutoff <- 1
pval_cutoff <- 0.05

for_plot <- deg_ttest %>%
  mutate(
    Expression = case_when(
      p_adj < pval_cutoff & lfc > logFC_cutoff & miRNA %in% diffexpressed_mirna$miRNA ~ "Upregulated",
      p_adj < pval_cutoff & lfc < -logFC_cutoff & miRNA %in% diffexpressed_mirna$miRNA ~ "Downregulated",
      TRUE ~ "Not significant"))

#Volcano plot of differentially expressed miRNAs
ggplot(for_plot, aes(x = lfc, y = -log10(p_adj), color = Expression)) +
  geom_point(size = 1.5, alpha = 0.7) +
  scale_color_manual( name = "miRNA expression" ,
  	values = c("Upregulated" = "#D6604D", "Downregulated" = "#2166AC", "Not significant" = "grey")) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = c(-logFC_cutoff, logFC_cutoff), linetype = "dashed") +
  geom_hline(yintercept = -log10(pval_cutoff), linetype = "dashed") +
  #geom_text(vjust = -1, size = 3) +
	geom_text_repel(aes(label = miRNA), size = 2, max.overlaps = 4) +
  theme_minimal() +
  labs(
    title = "",
    x = expression(paste(log[2], " Fold Change")),
    y = expression(paste("-", log[10], " p-value adjusted"))) + 
	xlim(-12, 4.5) + ylim(0, 3.2)

for_plot %>% summarise(n_upregulated = sum(Expression == "Upregulated"),
											 n_downregulated = sum(Expression == "Downregulated"),
											 total_DEGs = n_upregulated + n_downregulated) #%>% DT::datatable(caption = " Up and downregulated miRNA's DEG")
```

### Boxplots DEG miRNAs

Build in log scale or without? -- Better in regular counts, it's more visual.

All together, ordered by logFC (-,+)

```{r boxplots_deg_all, fig.height= 16, fig.width= 20, echo = F}
for_plot <- norm_data_cleaned[rownames(norm_data_cleaned) %in% (diffexpressed_mirna$miRNA), ]
for_plot <- for_plot %>%
  rownames_to_column("miRNA") %>% 
  pivot_longer(cols = -miRNA, names_to = "Sample", values_to = "Expression") %>% 
  mutate(Group = case_when(
    str_detect(Sample, "C") ~ "Control",
    str_detect(Sample, "D") ~ "FECD"
  )) %>% 
  group_by(miRNA, Group) %>%
  mutate(
    Q1 = quantile(Expression, 0.25),
    Q3 = quantile(Expression, 0.75),
    IQR = Q3 - Q1,
    is_outlier = ifelse(Expression < (Q1 - 1.5 * IQR) | Expression > (Q3 + 1.5 * IQR), T, F)
  ) %>%
  ungroup() %>% left_join(diffexpressed_mirna, by='miRNA') 


ggplot(for_plot,  aes(x = Group, y = Expression, fill = Group)) +
  geom_boxplot(show.legend = T)+
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("Control" = "#00BFC4", "FECD" = "#F8766D")) +
  geom_jitter(position=position_jitter(0.3), cex=1) +
  geom_text(
    data = filter(for_plot, is_outlier), aes(label = Sample),
    position = position_jitter(width = 0.2), vjust = -1, size = 1.5,
    color = "black") +
  
  theme(axis.text.x = element_blank(),
        plot.title = element_text(size = 30, hjust = 0.5),
        strip.text = element_text(size = 10)) +
  labs(
    title = "Differentially expressed miRNA", y = "Expression Level", x='') +
  facet_wrap(~reorder(miRNA, logFC), scales = "free")
```

UP and DOWN separately.

```{r fig.height= 16, fig.width= 20}
for_plot_long <- for_plot %>% filter(logFC < 0)
ggplot(for_plot_long,  aes(x = Group, y = Expression, fill = Group)) +
  geom_boxplot(show.legend = T)+
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("Control" = "#00BFC4", "FECD" = "#F8766D")) +
  geom_jitter(position=position_jitter(0.3), cex=1) +
  geom_text(
    data = filter(for_plot_long, is_outlier), aes(label = Sample),
    position = position_jitter(width = 0.2), vjust = -1, size = 1.5,
    color = "black") +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_blank(),
        plot.title = element_text(size = 20, hjust = 0.5),
        strip.text = element_text(size = 10)) +
  labs(y = "Expression Level", x='', title = 'DEGs downregulated') +
  facet_wrap(~reorder(miRNA, logFC), scales = "free")

for_plot_long <- for_plot %>% filter(logFC > 0)
ggplot(for_plot_long,  aes(x = Group, y = Expression, fill = Group)) +
  geom_boxplot(show.legend = T)+
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("Control" = "#00BFC4", "FECD" = "#F8766D")) +
  geom_jitter(position=position_jitter(0.3), cex=1) +
  geom_text(
    data = filter(for_plot_long, is_outlier), aes(label = Sample),
    position = position_jitter(width = 0.2), vjust = -1, size = 1.5,
    color = "black") +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_blank(),
        plot.title = element_text(size = 20, hjust = 0.5),
        strip.text = element_text(size = 10)) +
  labs(y = "Expression Level", x='', title = 'DEGs upwnregulated') +
  facet_wrap(~reorder(miRNA, logFC), scales = "free")

for_plot_long <- for_plot %>%
  group_by(miRNA, Sample) %>% arrange(desc(logFC)) %>% slice_head(n = 3) %>% ungroup() %>%
  bind_rows(
    for_plot %>% group_by(miRNA, Sample) %>% arrange(logFC) %>% slice_head(n = 3) %>% ungroup() ) %>% ungroup()

```

For article version

```{r }
top_miRNA <- for_plot %>% distinct(miRNA, logFC) %>% arrange(desc(logFC)) %>%
  {bind_rows(slice_head(., n = 8), slice_tail(., n = 8))} %>% pull(miRNA)              

for_plot_long <- for_plot %>% filter(miRNA %in% top_miRNA)

ggplot(for_plot_long,  aes(x = Group, y = Expression, fill = Group)) +
  geom_boxplot(show.legend = T, outliers = F)+
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("Control" = "#00BFC4", "FECD" = "#F8766D")) +
  geom_jitter(position=position_jitter(width = 0.3, height = 0),  size = 0.7, shape = 16, alpha = 0.7 ) +
# 	geom_text_repel(  
#     data = filter(for_plot_long, is_outlier),
#     aes(label = Sample),
#     size = 1.7, color = "black", box.padding = 0.1,   
#     point.padding = 0.3, min.segment.length = 0, segment.size = 0.3,      
#     segment.color = "grey50",
#     position = position_jitter(width = 0.2)) +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_blank(),
        axis.ticks.x = element_blank(),
        plot.title = element_text(size = 20, hjust = 0.5),
        strip.text = element_text(size = 7.2)) +
  labs(y = "Expression level (normalized counts)", x='') +
  facet_wrap(~reorder(miRNA, logFC), scales = "free") 
```

# ⭐Comparison with DEGs from another article

Let's compare our DEGs with the DEGs obtained earlier from another article on miRNA in FECD ([Article link](https://iovs.arvojournals.org/article.aspx?articleid=2128120)). List of DEGs is in suppl materials. Also add a table with a list of miRNAs from their array (TaqMan Open Array) and compare our arrays.

Are the data logarithmic? Let's fix this I thought they were logarithmic because there were negative values, but in fact this is not the case.

If FC = 0.5, that is, half as much, then it is the same as -2 times as much, that is, FC -2. To obtain such a value, you need $$negFC = -1 / FC$$To return them to the normal scale, i need to $-1/negFC$.

We need to bring them into logFC, and our accounts into negFC, compare both ways.

```{r matthaei_degs, echo=F}
matthaei_degs <- read.delim("/data7a/bio/human_genomics/fuchs_dystrophy/nanostring/analisys/upload/Matthaei_miRNA_DEGs.tsv") 

matthaei_degs <- matthaei_degs %>% filter(FoldChange != 'N/A') %>% 
  mutate(FoldChange = str_replace_all(FoldChange, ",", "")) %>% 
  as_tibble() %>%
  mutate(across(c(FoldChange, P.Value), as.numeric)) %>% 
  mutate(miRNA_raw = str_extract(Assay.Name_ID, "^[^_]+") %>%  
           str_replace_all(., "[#*]", "")) %>% na.omit() %>% 
  mutate(trueFC = if_else(FoldChange < 0, -1/FoldChange, FoldChange), 
         logFC = log2(trueFC), 
         Assay_ID = str_extract(Assay.Name_ID, "(?<=_)[^_]+$"))

# translate to hgnc
matthaei_degs <- matthaei_degs %>% mutate(
    HGNC_name = {
      x <- toupper(miRNA_raw) %>%
           gsub('HSA-', '', .) %>%
           gsub('-5P|-3P', '', .) %>%
           gsub('-', '', .)
      x
    },
    strand = ifelse(grepl("5P", toupper(miRNA_raw)), "5P",
                   ifelse(grepl("3P", toupper(miRNA_raw)), "3P", "5P")))

# add assay
taqman_assay <- read_excel("/data7a/bio/human_genomics/fuchs_dystrophy/nanostring/analisys/upload/matthei-assay-megaplex-pools-array-card-content.xlsx", sheet = 'Human A')

matthaei_degs <- matthaei_degs %>% left_join(taqman_assay, by= c('Assay_ID' = 'Assay ID')) %>% rename('miRNA' = 'miRBase ID (v22) or NCBI Name (for Controls)') %>% select(-`Control Assay`)


matthaei_degs_sign <- matthaei_degs %>% filter(P.Value <= 0.05, FoldChange <= -3)
saveRDS(matthaei_degs, file="/data7a/bio/human_genomics/fuchs_dystrophy/nanostring/analisys/RDS/matthaei_degs.RDS")
```

Let's intersect their essays with ours, then their essays with the DEGs from their article.

```{r, echo=F}
venn <- list(`Nanostring nCounter` = as.vector(rownames(norm_data)), `OpenArray Panel` = taqman_assay$`miRBase ID (v22) or NCBI Name (for Controls)`)
# miRNA intersection of Nanostring nCounter Human v3 miRNA Expression Assay with TaqMan OpenArray Human MicroRNA Panel
ggVennDiagram(venn, label = "count") + 
  #scale_fill_gradient(low = "#F4FAFE", high = "#4981BF") + 
  coord_flip() +
  labs(title = "miRNA intersection of Nanostring Expression Assay with TaqMan") + theme(legend.position="none") + 
  scale_y_continuous(expand = expansion(mult = .14)) +
  scale_fill_distiller(palette = "Blues", direction = 1)


venn <- list(Matt_assayID = matthaei_degs$Assay_ID, Matth_Assay = taqman_assay$`Assay ID`)
ggVennDiagram(venn) + 
  scale_fill_gradient(low = "#F4FAFE", high = "#4981BF") + coord_flip() +
  labs(title = "Matthei DEGs vs TaqMan assay all miRNA") + theme(legend.position="none") + scale_y_continuous(expand = expansion(mult = .14))

venn <- list(`Current study miRNA` = deg_ttest$miRNA, `Matthaei et. al DEGs` = matthaei_degs$miRNA )
ggVennDiagram(venn, label = "count") + 
  #scale_fill_gradient(low = "#F4FAFE", high = "#4981BF") + 
  coord_flip() +
  labs(title = "") + theme(legend.position="none")+ 
  scale_y_continuous(expand = expansion(mult = .14)) +
  scale_fill_distiller(palette = "Blues", direction = -1)
```

Intersection of our and their miRNA DEGs (100%)

```{r, echo=F}
venn <- list(`Our signif DEGs` = as.vector(diffexpressed_mirna$miRNA), `Matth all DEGs` = matthaei_degs$miRNA)

ggVennDiagram(venn) + scale_fill_gradient(low = "#F4FAFE", high = "#4981BF") + coord_flip() + labs(title = "Currents study and Matthaei DEGs intersection")

venn <- list(Nanostring = rownames(norm_data), Matth_DEGs = matthaei_degs$miRNA)

ggVennDiagram(venn) + scale_fill_gradient(low = "#F4FAFE", high = "#4981BF") + coord_flip() + 
  labs(title = "Intersection of our Nanostring assay with Matthei DEGs")
```

Fold change values distribution from both data.

```{r, echo=F}
ggplot() +
  geom_bar(aes(x = logFC , color = "matthaei_degs"), 
               data = matthaei_degs, linewidth = 1) + 
  geom_bar(aes(x = logFC, color = "diffexpressed_mirna"), 
               data = diffexpressed_mirna, linewidth = 0.6) +
  scale_color_manual(name = "Dataset",
                     values = c("matthaei_degs" = "blue", 
                               "diffexpressed_mirna" = "red")) + theme_minimal()

for_plot <- bind_rows(
  matthaei_degs %>% select(logFC) %>% mutate(Dataset = "Matthaei"),
  diffexpressed_mirna %>% select(logFC) %>% mutate(Dataset = "OurDEGS"))

ggplot(for_plot, aes(x = logFC, fill = Dataset)) +
  geom_density(alpha = 0.6, linewidth = 0.8) +
  facet_wrap(~ Dataset, ncol = 1, scales = "free") +  
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("Matthaei" = "blue", "OurDEGS" = "red")) +
  labs(x = "Log2 Fold Change", y = "Density") +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(legend.position = "none") 
```

## FC correlations scatter between Matt & Ours

Let us check whether the FCs of intersecting miRNAs correlate between DEGs and between all occurrences.

```{r fc_corr_plot1, echo=F}
deg_no_outl <- readRDS("/data7a/bio/human_genomics/fuchs_dystrophy/nanostring/analisys/RDS/deg_no_outl.RDS")

both_degs <- inner_join (
  diffexpressed_mirna %>%  mutate(Dataset = "OurDEGS"),  
                         matthaei_degs %>% mutate(Dataset = "Matthaei") %>%  select(miRNA, logFC, P.Value,Dataset, ), suffix = c(".our", ".matt"), by='miRNA')

title_name = "matching DEGS betw both articles"

ggplot(both_degs, aes(x = logFC.our, y = logFC.matt)) +
  geom_point(size = 5, alpha = 0.7) +
  geom_text_repel(aes(label = miRNA), size = 3, max.overlaps = 20) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", color = "gray50") +
  geom_vline(xintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", color = "gray50") +
  labs(x = "logFC (Our DEGs)", y = "logFC (Matthaei)",
    title = title_name, color = "miRNA") +
  theme_minimal() 
```

Let's try to take **all of our FCs, not just DEGs,** and pull the values ​​from the T-test (not from the limma, because it calculates them more cleverly). This way we will understand whether our data distributions are the same. We expect that they are. Note mir-29.

```{r corr_all_FC, echo=F}
# with all FC values + deg colouring
for_plot <- inner_join (
  deg_ttest %>%  mutate(Dataset = "OurDEGS"), 
  matthaei_degs %>% mutate(Dataset = "Matthaei") %>% select(-Assay.Name_ID), suffix = c(".our", ".matt"), by='miRNA') %>% 
  mutate(with_d = if_else(miRNA %in% diffexpressed_mirna$miRNA,  'DEG', 'no DEG'),
         dset = case_when(
miRNA %in% deg_no_outl$miRNA & miRNA %in% diffexpressed_mirna$miRNA ~ 'DEG was in no_outliers',
!(miRNA %in% deg_no_outl$miRNA) & miRNA %in% diffexpressed_mirna$miRNA ~ 'new DEGs',
!(miRNA %in% deg_no_outl$miRNA) & !(miRNA %in% diffexpressed_mirna$miRNA) ~ 'not DEG',
(miRNA %in% deg_no_outl$miRNA) & !(miRNA %in% diffexpressed_mirna$miRNA) ~ 'not DEG'
)) %>% 
  
  mutate(
    pl_size = if_else(miRNA %in% diffexpressed_mirna$miRNA | miRNA %in% matthaei_degs_sign$miRNA, 3,  0.5),
    # colour degs our mat no
    pl_col = case_when(
(miRNA %in% diffexpressed_mirna$miRNA & miRNA %in% matthaei_degs_sign$miRNA) ~ 'cyan',
miRNA %in% diffexpressed_mirna$miRNA ~ 'red',
miRNA %in% matthaei_degs_sign$miRNA ~ 'blue',
!(miRNA %in% diffexpressed_mirna$miRNA) & !(miRNA %in% matthaei_degs_sign$miRNA) ~ 'grey70') %>% as.factor(),
# set shape for mir29
pl_shape = if_else(str_detect(miRNA, 'miR-29') , 2, 19)
)


title_name = "logFC miRNA betw datasets, all our FC"
# total
ggplot(for_plot, aes(x = lfc, y = logFC, size = pl_size)) +
  geom_point( alpha = 0.6) + 
  geom_text_repel(aes(label = miRNA), size = 3, max.overlaps = 10) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", color = "gray50") +
  geom_vline(xintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", color = "gray50") +
  labs(x = "Our logFC for all MIR", y = "logFC Matthaei DEGs",
    title = title_name , color = "miRNA") +
  theme_minimal() 

ggplot(for_plot, aes(x = lfc, y = logFC, colour=with_d)) +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.5)  +
  geom_text_repel(aes(label = miRNA), size = 2, max.overlaps = 10) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", color = "gray50") +
  geom_vline(xintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", color = "gray50") +
  labs(x = "Our logFC for all MIR", y = "logFC Matthaei DEGs",
    title = title_name , color = "miRNA") + theme_minimal() 


# final
ggplot(for_plot, aes(x = lfc, y = logFC, size=pl_size, colour=pl_col, shape = pl_shape)) +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.5) + scale_size_identity() + # identity ест без фактора
  scale_shape_identity() +
  geom_text_repel(aes(label = miRNA), size = 2, max.overlaps = 10) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", color = "gray50") +
  geom_vline(xintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", color = "gray50") +
  labs(x = "Our logFC for all MIR", y = "logFC Matthaei DEGs",
    title = 'all' ) + theme_minimal() +
  scale_color_identity(guide = "legend",
    labels = c(
      "red" = "Our DEGs only",
      "blue" = "Matthaei DEGs only",
      "cyan" = "Both DEGs",
      "grey70" = "Not significant"
    ))
```

Let's do it differently: ours - theirs: blue pink, deg not deg is form. Let's take only the significant deg from them.

```{r fig.width=10, fig.height=7, echo=F}
for_plot <- inner_join (
  deg_ttest %>% mutate(Dataset = "OurDEGS"), 
  matthaei_degs %>% mutate(Dataset = "Matthaei") %>% select(-Assay.Name_ID), suffix = c(".our", ".matt"), by='miRNA') %>% 
  mutate(
    # our deg-not
    pl_size = if_else(miRNA %in% diffexpressed_mirna$miRNA,5,  2),
    # matt deg-not
    pl_col = if_else((miRNA %in% matthaei_degs_sign$miRNA), 'DEG','not DEG') %>% as.factor(),
pl_mir = if_else(str_detect(miRNA, 'miR-29[abc]'), miRNA, NA_character_),
shape_mir = if_else(str_detect(miRNA, 'miR-29[abc]'), 2, 19)
)


ggplot(for_plot, aes(x = lfc, y = logFC,  size = pl_size,  colour=pl_col, shape=shape_mir) )+
  geom_point(alpha = 0.7) +   # identity ест без фактора
  geom_text_repel(aes(label = if_else(is.na(pl_mir), miRNA, NA_character_ )), size = 2.5, max.overlaps = 3, segment.color = "grey50") +
 
  geom_vline(xintercept = 1, linetype = "dashed", color = "gray50") +
  geom_vline(xintercept = -1, linetype = "dashed", color = "gray50") +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 3, linetype = "dashed", color = "gray50") +
  geom_hline(yintercept = -3, linetype = "dashed", color = "gray50") +
	
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0,  color = "black") +
	geom_vline(xintercept = 0, color = "black") +
	
  labs(x = expression("miRNA" ~ log[2]("FC") ~ "current study"), 
  		 y = expression("miRNA" ~ log[2]("FC") ~ " Matthaei et. al., 2014"),
    title = '') + theme_minimal() +
  scale_size_identity(
    guide = "legend",
    breaks = c(5, 2),
    labels = c("DEG", "not DEG" ),
    name = "Current study") +
  
  geom_text_repel(
    aes(label = if_else(is.na(pl_mir), NA_character_, miRNA)), 
    segment.color = "grey50", segment.size = 3.5, box.padding = 0.3,
    size = 2.2, colour='black', max.overlaps = 3, na.rm = T, max.time = 5.5) +
  scale_color_manual(name = "Matthaei et. al., 2014",
                     values = c("DEG" = "#1f77b4",  
                               "not DEG" = "#ff7f0e"))  +xlim(-7, 2.5) + scale_shape_identity() 



both_degs <- for_plot %>% filter(pl_size == 5 & pl_col == 'DEG') 
both_degs #%>% DT::datatable(caption='Both DEGS Matthei & Current', filter = 'top')

final_FECD_MIMAT <- readRDS("/data7a/bio/human_genomics/fuchs_dystrophy/nanostring/analisys/RDS/final_FECD_MIMAT_v3.RDS")

final_FECD_MIMAT %>% filter(miRNA %in% both_degs$miRNA) #%>% DT::datatable(caption = 'Both DEGs presented in downcorr pairs')
```

compare FC from limma vs FC from classic way. – They're almost the same.

```{r fc_difference}
for_plot <- left_join(deg_limma, deg_ttest , by='miRNA') %>% 
  rename(manual_FC = lfc, limma_FC = logFC) %>% pivot_longer(cols = c(manual_FC, limma_FC), names_to = 'Test', values_to = 'logFC') 

ggplot(for_plot, aes(x=reorder(miRNA, logFC), y=(logFC), fill=Test)) +
  geom_col(position = position_dodge(width = 0.8), width = 0.7)

ggplot(for_plot, aes( x=(logFC), fill=Test)) + geom_density(alpha=0.4)
```

```{r get_annot, echo=F}
chip_mimat <- readRDS("/data7a/bio/human_genomics/fuchs_dystrophy/nanostring/analisys/RDS/chip_mimat.RDS")
mimat_table <- readRDS("/data7a/bio/human_genomics/fuchs_dystrophy/nanostring/analisys/RDS/mimat_table.RDS")
```

# Сonfirm the expression through CPM {.tabset}

Let's confirm expression using CPM: plot a scatterplot of the correlation between counts (not just DEGs) from the assay and counts from CPM. If it's positive, the counts are not artifactual.

CPM contains some values ​​for both the miRNA and its gene (HG - host gene). We'll look at the expression of the host genes present in our dataset. We need to plot this on all miRNAs, not just HG.

```{r add_coord_beds, echo=F}
mirtrones <- read_delim('/data7a/bio/human_genomics/fuchs_dystrophy/nanostring/analisys/download/annot_chipdf_genes_s_y.bed', delim = '\t', col_names = c("seqnames", "start", "end", "gene_id","gene_name", "strand","type", "gene_type", "seqnames_mir", "start_mir", "end_mir", "gene_id_mir","gene_name_mir", "strand_mir","type_mir"))
mirtrones <- mirtrones %>% filter(type == 'gene', gene_type == 'protein_coding')

mirtrones_mimat <- read_delim('/data7a/bio/human_genomics/fuchs_dystrophy/nanostring/analisys/download/chip_mimat_s_y.bed', delim = '\t', col_names = c("seqnames", "start", "end", "gene_id","gene_name", "strand","type", "gene_type", "seqnames_mir", "start_mir", "end_mir", "gene_id_mir","gene_name_mir", "strand_mir","Alias", "ID", "Derives_from",    "type_mir")) %>% filter(type == 'gene', gene_type == 'protein_coding')

mirtrones_matthaei <- read_delim('/data7a/bio/human_genomics/fuchs_dystrophy/nanostring/analisys/download/matthaei_mimat_s_y.bed', delim = '\t', col_names = c("seqnames", "start", "end", "gene_id","gene_name", "strand","type", "gene_type", "seqnames_mir", "start_mir", "end_mir", "gene_id_mir","gene_name_mir", "strand_mir", "mimat_Alias", "mimat_ID", "Derives_from",   "uniq_pos")) %>% filter(type == 'gene', gene_type == 'protein_coding')
```

### All our host genes in CPM corrs

```{r get_cpm_our, echo=T}
cpm.symbol <- readRDS("/data7a/bio/human_genomics/fuchs_dystrophy/illumina/Fuchs_transcriptomes/analysis/transcript_analysis/RDS/expression/cpm.symbol_list.RDS")
cpm.symbol <- cpm.symbol[[1]] %>% dplyr::select(-c('C_209', 'C_210', 'C_211','Dfu_209', 'Dfu_223', 'Dfu_210'))

grep("MIR", rownames(cpm.symbol), value = TRUE)
cpm.symbol_hg <- cpm.symbol
cpm.symbol_hg <- cpm.symbol_hg[grep("MIR", rownames(cpm.symbol_hg)) ,]
# похожа ли mir с её HG? - Да, remove it
cor(as.numeric(cpm.symbol_hg['MIR663A', ]), as.numeric(cpm.symbol_hg['MIR663AHG', ]))
cpm.symbol_hg <- cpm.symbol_hg[rownames(cpm.symbol_hg) != "MIR663A", , drop = F]

rownames(cpm.symbol_hg) <- str_remove(rownames(cpm.symbol_hg), "HG")
```

What are the Host Genes on matthaei degs mRNA in our CPMs?

```{r hg_in_matth}
intersect(matthaei_degs$HGNC_name, rownames(cpm.symbol_hg))
```

```{r, echo=F}
# add rnaseq expression counts 
for_plot <- cpm.symbol_hg %>%
  rownames_to_column("gene") %>% 
  pivot_longer(cols = -gene, names_to = "Sample_name", values_to = "gene_expression") 

# add miRNA counts с аннотацией
for_merge <- norm_data_cleaned %>%
  rownames_to_column("miRNA") %>%  
  pivot_longer(cols = -miRNA, names_to = "Sample_name", values_to = "mirna_expression") %>% 
  left_join(chip_mimat %>% select(miRNA, HGNC_name), by = 'miRNA')

for_plot <- left_join(for_merge, for_plot, by = c('HGNC_name' = 'gene', 'Sample_name' = 'Sample_name')) %>% na.omit() %>% 
  mutate(log_mirna_expression = log2(mirna_expression),
         log_gene_expression = gene_expression,
         gene_expression = 2^gene_expression) #de-log
```

Plot of all intersecting miRNAs (not just DEGs) in the Nanostring chip and their host gene from CPM with lm line and spearman correlation

log CPM vs de-log miRNA counts

```{r, echo=F, fig.height=6, fig.width=9}
cor_data <- for_plot %>%
  filter(str_detect(Sample_name, "C")) %>% group_by(miRNA) %>%
  summarise(
    cor = cor(gene_expression, mirna_expression, method = "spearman"),
    cor_label = paste0("r = ", round(cor, 2))) %>% 
    mutate(x_pos = -Inf,  y_pos = Inf)

ggplot(for_plot %>%  filter(str_detect(Sample_name, "C")), aes(x=log_gene_expression, y=mirna_expression)) + geom_point() +   labs(title="Control: de-log miRNA from array and miRNA's Host Genes log2 CPM genes expression") + facet_wrap(~miRNA, scales = 'free') +
  geom_smooth(method = "lm", se = F, color = "blue", linewidth=0.5, linetype='dashed') +
  geom_text(data = cor_data, 
    aes(x = x_pos, y = y_pos, label = cor_label),
    color = "blue", size = 3, hjust = -0.3, vjust = 1.4)

# fecd
cor_data <- for_plot %>%
  filter(str_detect(Sample_name, "D")) %>% group_by(miRNA) %>%
  summarise(
    cor = cor(gene_expression, mirna_expression, method = "spearman"),
    cor_label = paste0("r = ", round(cor, 2))) %>% 
    mutate(x_pos = -Inf,  y_pos = Inf)

ggplot(for_plot %>%  filter(str_detect(Sample_name, "D")), aes(x=log_gene_expression, y= mirna_expression)) + geom_point() +   labs(title="FECD: de-log miRNA from array and miRNA's Host Genes log CPM genes expression") + facet_wrap(~miRNA, scales = 'free') +
  geom_smooth(method = "lm", se = F, color = "blue", linewidth=0.5, linetype='dashed') +
  geom_text(data = cor_data, 
    aes(x = x_pos, y = y_pos, label = cor_label),
    color = "blue", size = 3, hjust = -0.3, vjust = 1.4)
```

### all our mirtrones

```{r forplot_corrs_allmirtrones, echo=F}

# add cpm expressiob
for_plot <- cpm.symbol %>%
  rownames_to_column("gene") %>% 
  pivot_longer(cols = -gene, names_to = "Sample_name", values_to = "gene_expression") 

# add miRNA counts
for_merge <- norm_data_cleaned %>%
  rownames_to_column("miRNA") %>%  
  pivot_longer(cols = -miRNA, names_to = "Sample_name", values_to = "mirna_expression") %>% 
  left_join(mirtrones_mimat %>% select(gene_name, gene_id_mir, type_mir, Alias, ID), by = c('miRNA' = 'gene_id_mir'))

for_plot <- left_join(for_merge, for_plot, by = c('gene_name' = 'gene', 'Sample_name' = 'Sample_name')) %>% na.omit() %>% 
  mutate(log_mirna_expression = log2(mirna_expression),
         log_gene_expression = gene_expression,
         gene_expression = 2^gene_expression) %>% rename(in_gene = gene_name) %>% #de-log 
  mutate(MIR_gene = str_glue("{miRNA}\n({in_gene})"))
```

### Our DEGs mirtrones expression corr

Let's build the same thing, but using our DEGs and their mirtrons, not all host genes. LogFC order is (-inf, +inf).

```{r deg_mirtr_cor, echo=F, fig.height=10, fig.width=12}
# subset degs, order
for_plot <- for_plot %>% filter(miRNA %in% diffexpressed_mirna$miRNA) %>% 
  left_join( diffexpressed_mirna %>% select(miRNA, logFC), by='miRNA') %>% 
  mutate(logFC_direction = ifelse(logFC > 0, "Upregulated", "Downregulated"))


# check same mirnas in multi genes
mir_dups <- for_plot %>% group_by(miRNA) %>%  summarise(
    unique_gene_count = n_distinct(in_gene))  %>%
  filter(unique_gene_count > 1)  %>% pull(miRNA) #%T>% 
  #{DT::datatable(., caption = "MIR found in multiple genes") %>% print()}  %>% pull(miRNA) 


facet_order <- for_plot %>% distinct(MIR_gene, logFC) %>% arrange((logFC)) %>% pull(MIR_gene)

for_plot <- for_plot %>%  mutate(MIR_gene = factor(MIR_gene, levels = facet_order)) # for order

#control
for_plot_long <- for_plot %>%  filter(str_detect(Sample_name, "C"))
cor_data <- for_plot_long %>% group_by(MIR_gene) %>%
  summarise(
    cor = cor(gene_expression, mirna_expression, method = "spearman"),
    cor_label = paste0("r = ", round(cor, 2))) %>% 
    mutate(x_pos = -Inf,  y_pos = Inf)

ggplot(for_plot_long, aes(x=gene_expression, y= mirna_expression, color = logFC_direction )) + 
  geom_point() + 
  labs(title="Control: our DEG miRNA and its mirtrones CPM expression (de-log)") + facet_wrap(~MIR_gene, scales = 'free') +
  geom_smooth(method = "lm", se = F, color = "blue", linewidth=0.5, linetype='dashed') +
  geom_text(data = cor_data, 
    aes(x = x_pos, y = y_pos, label = cor_label),
    color = "blue", size = 3, hjust = -0.3, vjust = 1.4) 

# fecd
for_plot_long <- for_plot %>%  filter(str_detect(Sample_name, "D"))
cor_data <- for_plot_long %>% group_by(MIR_gene) %>%
  summarise(
    cor = cor(gene_expression, mirna_expression, method = "spearman"),
    cor_label = paste0("r = ", round(cor, 2))) %>% 
    mutate(x_pos = -Inf,  y_pos = Inf)

ggplot(for_plot_long, aes(x=gene_expression, y= mirna_expression, color = logFC_direction)) + 
  geom_point() +
  labs(title="FECD: our DEG miRNA and its mirtrones CPM expression (de-log)") + facet_wrap(~MIR_gene, scales = 'free') +
  geom_smooth(method = "lm", se = F, color = "blue", linewidth=0.5, linetype='dashed') +
  geom_text(data = cor_data, 
    aes(x = x_pos, y = y_pos, label = cor_label),
    color = "blue", size = 3, hjust = -0.3, vjust = 1.4) 
```

Let's make a cropped chart with correlations for the article \~ 8x8.

```{r fig.height=7, fig.width=7}
# title="FECD: our DEG miRNA and its mirtrones CPM expression (de-log)"

selected_groups <- for_plot_long %>%
  group_by(MIR_gene) %>%
  summarise(mean_logFC = mean(logFC, na.rm = TRUE)) %>%
  arrange(desc(mean_logFC)) %>%
  {bind_rows(slice_head(., n = 8), slice_tail(., n = 8))} %>%
  pull(MIR_gene) %>% as.character()

for_plot_long2 <- for_plot_long %>% filter(MIR_gene %in% selected_groups)


ggplot(for_plot_long2, aes(x=gene_expression, y= mirna_expression, color = logFC_direction)) + 
  geom_point() +
  facet_wrap(~MIR_gene, scales = 'free') +
  geom_smooth(method = "lm", se = F, color = "blue", linewidth=0.5, linetype='dashed') +
  geom_text(data = cor_data %>% filter(MIR_gene %in% for_plot_long2$MIR_gene), 
    aes(x = x_pos, y = y_pos, label = cor_label),
    color = "blue", size = 3, hjust = -0.3, vjust = 1.4) +
  
  labs(y='miRNA expression in normalized counts', x='Gene expression in CPM',
       color = "DE miRNA") + 
  theme(legend.position = "bottom",
       legend.box = "horizontal")
```

Correlations Fecd, Control on same plot

```{r fig.height=12, fig.width=14}

for_plot_long <- for_plot %>%
  mutate(group = if_else(str_detect(Sample_name, "C"), "Control", "FECD"))

cor_data <- for_plot_long %>%
  group_by(MIR_gene, group) %>%
  summarise(
    cor = cor(gene_expression, mirna_expression, method = "spearman"),
    cor_label = paste0("r = ", round(cor, 2)),
    .groups = 'drop'
  ) %>%
  mutate(x_pos = -Inf,  y_pos = Inf)


ggplot(for_plot_long, aes(x = gene_expression, y = mirna_expression)) + 
  geom_point(aes(color = group, shape = logFC_direction), 
             size = 3, alpha = 0.7) + 
  
  geom_smooth(aes(color = group, linetype = group), 
              method = "lm", se = FALSE, linewidth = 0.8) +
  
  # Подписи корреляций
  geom_text(data = cor_data, 
            aes(x = x_pos, y = y_pos, label = cor_label, color = group),
            hjust = -0.1, vjust = 1.5, size = 3.5, show.legend = FALSE) +
  
  scale_color_manual(values = c("Control" = "#1f77b4", "FECD" = "#ff7f0e")) +
  scale_shape_manual(values = c("Upregulated" = 16, "Downregulated" = 17)) +
  scale_linetype_manual(values = c("Control" = "dashed", "FECD" = "dashed")) +
  
  labs(
    title = "miRNA vs Gene Expression Correlation",
    subtitle = "Control (blue) vs FECD (orange) groups",
    x = "Gene Expression (CPM)",
    y = "miRNA Expression",
    color = "Group",
    shape = "LogFC Direction",
    linetype = "Group"
  ) +
  theme_bw() +
  theme(
    strip.text = element_text(size = 9),
    legend.position = "bottom",
    legend.box = "horizontal",
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank()
  ) +
  guides(
    color = guide_legend(order = 1),
    shape = guide_legend(order = 2),
    linetype = guide_legend(order = 1)) +
	  facet_wrap(~ MIR_gene, scales = 'free') 
```

Также построим бокспоты по каждой миРНК с экспрессией семплов в чипе и CPM в лог и не лог.

```{r box_mircpm_control, echo=F, eval=F}
for_plot_long <- for_plot %>%
  pivot_longer(
    cols = c(log_gene_expression, log_mirna_expression),
    names_to = "expression_type",
    values_to = "log_value_expression"
  ) %>%
  mutate(expression_type = factor(
    expression_type,
    levels = c("log_gene_expression", "log_mirna_expression"),
    labels = c("Gene Expression", "miRNA Expression")
  ))


ggplot(for_plot_long %>%  filter(str_detect(Sample_name, "C")), aes(x = expression_type, y = log_value_expression, fill = expression_type)) + geom_boxplot() +   
  geom_jitter(position=position_jitter(0.3), size = 0.5) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("#00BFC4", "#F8766D")) +
  labs(title="Control: log miRNA from array and miRNA's CPM genes expression", x='') +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_blank(),
        plot.title = element_text(size = 12, hjust = 0.5),
        strip.text = element_text(size = 6)) +
  facet_wrap(~miRNA, scales = "free")

for_plot_long <- for_plot %>%
  pivot_longer(
    cols = c(gene_expression, mirna_expression),
    names_to = "expression_type",
    values_to = "value_expression"
  ) %>%
  mutate(expression_type = factor(
    expression_type,
    levels = c("gene_expression", "mirna_expression"),
    labels = c("Gene Expression", "miRNA Expression")
  ))


ggplot(for_plot_long %>%  filter(str_detect(Sample_name, "C")), aes(x = expression_type, y = value_expression, fill = expression_type)) + geom_boxplot() +   
  geom_jitter(position=position_jitter(0.3), size = 0.5) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("#00BFC4", "#F8766D")) +
  labs(title="Control: miRNA from array and miRNA's CPM genes expression", x='') +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_blank(),
        plot.title = element_text(size = 12, hjust = 0.5),
        strip.text = element_text(size = 6)) + # name on tile
  facet_wrap(~miRNA, scales = "free")
```

Теперь по Fecd

```{r box_mircpm_fecd, echo=F, eval=F}
for_plot_long <- for_plot %>%
  pivot_longer(
    cols = c(log_gene_expression, log_mirna_expression),
    names_to = "expression_type",
    values_to = "log_value_expression"
  ) %>%
  mutate(expression_type = factor(
    expression_type,
    levels = c("log_gene_expression", "log_mirna_expression"),
    labels = c("Gene Expression", "miRNA Expression")
  ))


ggplot(for_plot_long %>%  filter(str_detect(Sample_name, "D")), aes(x = expression_type, y = log_value_expression, fill = expression_type)) + geom_boxplot() +   
  geom_jitter(position=position_jitter(0.3), size = 0.5) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("#00BFC4", "#F8766D")) +
  labs(title="FECD: log miRNA from array and miRNA's CPM genes expression", x='') +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_blank(),
        plot.title = element_text(size = 12, hjust = 0.5),
        strip.text = element_text(size = 6)) +
  facet_wrap(~miRNA, scales = "free")

for_plot_long <- for_plot %>%
  pivot_longer(
    cols = c(gene_expression, mirna_expression),
    names_to = "expression_type",
    values_to = "value_expression"
  ) %>%
  mutate(expression_type = factor(
    expression_type,
    levels = c("gene_expression", "mirna_expression"),
    labels = c("Gene Expression", "miRNA Expression")
  ))


ggplot(for_plot_long %>%  filter(str_detect(Sample_name, "D")), aes(x = expression_type, y = value_expression, fill = expression_type)) + geom_boxplot() +   
  geom_jitter(position=position_jitter(0.3), size = 0.5) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("#00BFC4", "#F8766D")) +
  labs(title="FECD: miRNA from array and miRNA's CPM genes expression", x='') +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_blank(),
        plot.title = element_text(size = 12, hjust = 0.5),
        strip.text = element_text(size = 6)) + # name on tile
  facet_wrap(~miRNA, scales = "free")
```

## CPM Boxplots for DEGs: our, nar

We need to build boxes only for DEGs: separately for our up and down and for the Nar set. For each up: our Fecd, Control; Nar's Fecd, Control, for a total of 4 boxes.

> Для наши х ап и даун отдельно бокс плот наш fexd, наш контрол, нар фекд, нар контроль прекурсора

```{r get_cpm_nar, echo=T}
nar <- readRDS("/data7a/bio/human_genomics/fuchs_dystrophy/illumina/Fuchs_transcriptomes/analysis/transcript_analysis/RDS/expression/cpm.symbol_list.RDS")
nar <- nar[[3]] 

grep("MIR", rownames(nar), value = TRUE)
nar <- nar[grep("MIR", rownames(nar)) ,]
# похожа ли mir с её HG? - Да, remove it
cor(as.numeric(nar['MIR663A', ]), as.numeric(nar['MIR663AHG', ]))
nar_hg <- nar[rownames(nar) != "MIR663A", , drop = F]

# оставим для добавление 2х мирнк генов
rownames(nar_hg) <- str_remove(rownames(nar_hg), "HG")
```

Not all host genes are in CPM, but many miRNAs don't have their own gene because they are mirtrones, meaning their host gene is some protein-coding gene. I previously intersected them with bedtools, so now let's try using these genes. Let's add a strand-specific bed with such intersections: `annot_chipdf_genes_s_y.bed`. There's a transcript level there, and it would be better to select its sum as gene expression values, but this is probably fine.

Конечно мы потеряем -5P/3P, тк будем вытягивать по HGNC названию

```{r again_cpm, echo=F}
nar <- readRDS("/data7a/bio/human_genomics/fuchs_dystrophy/illumina/Fuchs_transcriptomes/analysis/transcript_analysis/RDS/expression/cpm.symbol_list.RDS") [[3]] %>% select(-FECD_NREP3 )
cpm.symbol <- readRDS("/data7a/bio/human_genomics/fuchs_dystrophy/illumina/Fuchs_transcriptomes/analysis/transcript_analysis/RDS/expression/cpm.symbol_list.RDS")
cpm.symbol <- cpm.symbol[[1]] %>% select(-c('C_209', 'C_210', 'Dfu_210', 'C_211','Dfu_209', 'Dfu_223'))
cpm.symbol <- 2^cpm.symbol
nar <- 2^nar
```

те 2 найденные в спм плотить отдельно или как-либо встроить сюда? Те 2 ДЭГ мирнки, на которых есть host gene в полученной таблице отсутствуют. сначала к ней подтянуть, а потом к остальным миртроны. добавил вручную их HG

попробуем сразу сделать cpm с нужными генами

```{r our_nar_mirtrones_deg_hgnc, eval=F}
# colors for box
my_colors <- c("nar control" = "#338050", "nar fecd" = "#A83232", "our control" = "#78C679", "our fecd" = "#FF6B6B")

# all mirtrones
for_plot <- diffexpressed_mirna_HGNC %>% 
  left_join(
    mirtrones %>% select(gene_name, gene_name_mir), by = c('HGNC_symbol' = 'gene_name_mir')) %>% 
  #filter(!is.na(gene_name)) %>% 
  rename(in_gene = gene_name) %>% 
  mutate(
    in_gene = case_when(
      HGNC_symbol == "MIR1915" ~ "MIR1915HG", 
      HGNC_symbol =="MIR30D" ~ "MIR30DHG", 
      TRUE ~ in_gene)) %>% 
  left_join(nar %>% rownames_to_column('in_gene'), by='in_gene') %>% 
  left_join(cpm.symbol %>% rownames_to_column('in_gene'), by='in_gene') %>% 
  left_join(diffexpressed_mirna %>% select(miRNA, logFC), by='miRNA') %>% 
  filter(!is.na(in_gene)) %>% filter(!is.na(Ctrl0)) %>%  # убрать которых нет в спм 
  mutate(MIR_gene = str_glue("{miRNA}\n({in_gene})"))

```

Пересечём с mimat миртронами. плохо, что я не добавил оба mimat id когда пересекал.

Есть ли среди наших ДЭГов разные миРНК, которые лежат внутри одинакового гена -- есть 1 миРНК, с разным суффиксом -5р\\3р. Есть ли миРНК, которые лежат внутри нескольких генов?

```{r our_nar_mirtrones_deg_mimat}

for_plot <- diffexpressed_mirna_HGNC %>% 
  left_join(
    mirtrones_mimat %>% select(gene_name, gene_id_mir, seqnames), by = c('miRNA' = 'gene_id_mir')) %>% 
  #filter(!is.na(gene_name)) %>% 
  rename(in_gene = gene_name) %>% 
  mutate(
    in_gene = case_when(
      HGNC_symbol == "MIR1915" ~ "MIR1915HG", 
      HGNC_symbol =="MIR30D" ~ "MIR30DHG", 
      TRUE ~ in_gene)) %>% 
  left_join(nar %>% rownames_to_column('in_gene'), by='in_gene') %>% 
  left_join(cpm.symbol %>% rownames_to_column('in_gene'), by='in_gene') %>% 
  left_join(diffexpressed_mirna %>% select(miRNA, logFC), by='miRNA') %>% 
  filter(!is.na(in_gene)) %>% filter(!is.na(Ctrl0)) %>%  # убрать которых нет в спм 
  mutate(MIR_gene = str_glue("{miRNA}\n({in_gene})"))


# same genes
for_plot %>% group_by(in_gene) %>% summarise(
    unique_mirna_count = n_distinct(miRNA))  %>%
  filter(unique_mirna_count > 1) #%>% DT::datatable(caption = "Mirtrone genes with multiple miRNA in them")

for_plot %>% group_by(miRNA) %>% summarise(
    unique_mirna_count = n_distinct(in_gene))  %>%
  filter(unique_mirna_count > 1) #%>% DT::datatable(caption = "MIR found in multiple genes")


# rm 
for_plot <- for_plot %>% filter(!(miRNA %in% c('hsa-miR-103a-3p', 'hsa-miR-1285-5p')))
```

**Разделим по значению logFC:**

-   UP -- В Fecd её больше, чем в Control

-   DOWN - в Control больше чем в Fecd

-   Следовательно host genes миртронов должны обладать той же зависимостью

Поменять шкалу free на фикс

```{r boxplot_our_nar_down, echo = F, fig.width=12, fig.height=8}
# colors for box
my_colors <- c("nar control" = "#338050", "nar fecd" = "#A83232", "our control" = "#78C679", "our fecd" = "#FF6B6B")

facet_order <- for_plot %>% distinct(MIR_gene, logFC) %>% 
  arrange((logFC)) %>% pull(MIR_gene)

for_plot_long <- for_plot %>% filter(logFC < 0) %>%
  mutate(MIR_gene = factor(MIR_gene, levels = facet_order)) %>% # for order
  pivot_longer(
    cols = -c(miRNA, HGNC_symbol, strand, MIMAT_ID, in_gene, logFC, MIR_gene,seqnames),
    names_to = "sample_group", values_to = "expression") %>%
  
  mutate(
    data_type = case_when(
      grepl("^Ctrl|^FECD", sample_group) ~ "nar",
      grepl("^C_|^Dfu", sample_group) ~ "our",
      TRUE ~ NA_character_),
    group = case_when(
      grepl("^Ctrl|^C_", sample_group) ~ "control",
      grepl("^FECD|^Dfu", sample_group) ~ "fecd",
      TRUE ~ NA_character_)) %>%
  mutate(full_group = paste(data_type, group)) 



# rm omit%>% na.omit()
ggplot(for_plot_long , aes(x = full_group, y = expression, fill = full_group)) +
  geom_boxplot() +
  geom_jitter(position=position_jitter(0.3), size= 0.1) +
  facet_wrap(~ MIR_gene, scales = 'free') +
  labs(
    title = "Downreg DEG mirna by mirtrones genes expression (FC: -inf -> 0)",
    y = "Expression Level") +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_blank(),
        plot.title = element_text(size = 30, hjust = 0.5),
        strip.text = element_text(size = 16)) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = my_colors)

```

Up. (сделать тут от inf до 0)

```{r boxplot_our_nar_up, echo = F, fig.width=14, fig.height=9}

facet_order <- for_plot %>% distinct(MIR_gene, logFC) %>% 
  arrange(desc(logFC)) %>% pull(MIR_gene)

for_plot_long <- for_plot %>% filter(logFC > 0) %>%
  mutate(MIR_gene = factor(MIR_gene, levels = facet_order)) %>% # for order
  pivot_longer(
    cols = -c(miRNA, HGNC_symbol, strand, MIMAT_ID, in_gene, logFC, MIR_gene,seqnames),
    names_to = "sample_group",
    values_to = "expression") %>%
  mutate(
    data_type = case_when(
      grepl("^Ctrl|^FECD", sample_group) ~ "nar",
      grepl("^C_|^Dfu", sample_group) ~ "our",
      TRUE ~ NA_character_),
    group = case_when(
      grepl("^Ctrl|^C_", sample_group) ~ "control",
      grepl("^FECD|^Dfu", sample_group) ~ "fecd",
      TRUE ~ NA_character_)) %>%
  mutate(full_group = paste(data_type, group)) 

ggplot(for_plot_long  , aes(x = full_group, y = expression, fill = full_group)) +
  geom_boxplot() +
  geom_jitter(position=position_jitter(0.3), size= 0.1) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = my_colors) +
  facet_wrap(~ MIR_gene, scales = 'free') +
  labs(
    title = "Upreg DEG mirna by host genes expression (FC: inf->0 )",
    y = "Expression Level") +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_blank(),
        plot.title = element_text(size = 30, hjust = 0.5),
        strip.text = element_text(size = 11)) 
```

сделаем все для статьи на 1 картинке снова 8х8

```{r boxplot_our_nar_all, echo = F, fig.width=10, fig.height=9}

my_colors <- c("Chu et al. Control" = "#338050", "Chu et al. FECD" = "#A83232", "Nikitina et al. Control" = "#78C679", "Nikitina et al. FECD" = "#FF6B6B")

for_plot_long <- for_plot %>%
  arrange(desc(logFC)) %>%
  {bind_rows(slice_head(., n = 8), slice_tail(., n = 8))} #%>%
  #pull(MIR_gene) %>% as.character()

facet_order <- for_plot_long %>% distinct(MIR_gene, logFC) %>% 
  arrange((logFC)) %>% pull(MIR_gene)

for_plot_long <- for_plot_long %>%
  mutate(MIR_gene = factor(MIR_gene, levels = facet_order)) %>% # for order
  pivot_longer(
    cols = -c(miRNA, HGNC_symbol, strand, MIMAT_ID, in_gene, logFC, MIR_gene,seqnames),
    names_to = "sample_group",
    values_to = "expression") %>%
  mutate(
    data_type = case_when(
      grepl("^Ctrl|^FECD", sample_group) ~ "Chu et al.",
      grepl("^C_|^Dfu", sample_group) ~ "Nikitina et al.",
      TRUE ~ NA_character_),
    group = case_when(
      grepl("^Ctrl|^C_", sample_group) ~ "Control",
      grepl("^FECD|^Dfu", sample_group) ~ "FECD",
      TRUE ~ NA_character_)) %>%
  mutate(Dataset = paste(data_type, group)) 

ggplot(for_plot_long, aes(x = Dataset, y = expression, fill = Dataset)) +
  geom_boxplot(outliers = F) +
  geom_jitter(position=position_jitter(0.3), size= 0.1) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = my_colors) +
  facet_wrap(~ MIR_gene, scales = 'free') +
  labs(
    y = "Gene expression in CPM", x='') +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_blank(), axis.ticks.x = element_blank(),
        plot.title = element_text(size = 30, hjust = 0.5),
        strip.text = element_text(size = 13), 
        legend.position = "bottom", 
        legend.text=element_text(size=13),
        legend.title=element_text(size=14),
    legend.box = "horizontal",    #panel.grid.minor = element_blank()
  ) 
```

```{r}
for_plot_long <- for_plot_long %>%
  mutate(
    GroupSet = ifelse(MIR_gene %in% head(facet_order, 8), "Bottom 8", "Top 8")
  )

ggplot(for_plot_long, aes(x = Dataset, y = expression, fill = Dataset)) +
  geom_boxplot(outliers = F) +
  geom_jitter(position = position_jitter(0.3), size = 0.1) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = my_colors) +
  facet_grid(GroupSet ~ MIR_gene, scales = "free", space = "free_x") +
  labs(y = "Gene expression in CPM", x='') +
  theme(
    axis.text.x = element_blank(), 
    axis.ticks.x = element_blank(),
    plot.title = element_text(size = 30, hjust = 0.5),
    strip.text = element_text(size = 13), 
    legend.position = "bottom", 
    legend.text=element_text(size=13),
    legend.title=element_text(size=14),
    legend.box = "horizontal"
  )

```

# Проверим FC дэгов наши и из статьи на CPM сетах {.tabset}

Сравниваем FC их DEG мирнк с экспрессией их миртрон-генов (которые я получил пересечением bed) в обоих датасетах: nar, our. Так мы сможем понять, насколько их Fold Change коррелируют с нашими данными. То же самое потом и на наших DEG.

Добавим к названиям генов из статьи экспрессию их генов. Сначала я добавлял из пересечения нашей таблицы против аннотации без миРНК, потом я отдельно пересёк с ней миРНК гены из статьи, получились те же гены, хотя в пересечении с аннотацией было всего 80/120 вхождений.

```{r matt_logs_fc_all, echo=F}
logfc_our <- log2(
  cpm.symbol %>% select(starts_with("Dfu_")) %>% rowMeans() / cpm.symbol %>% select(starts_with("C_")) %>% rowMeans()) %>% as.data.frame() %>% setNames("our_logFC") %>% rownames_to_column('gene') #%>% mutate(rank =rank(our_logFC)) 

logfc_nar <- log2(
  nar %>% select(starts_with("FECD")) %>% rowMeans() / nar %>% select(starts_with("Ctrl"))  %>% rowMeans()) %>% as.data.frame() %>% setNames("nar_logFC") %>% rownames_to_column('gene') #%>% mutate(rank = rank(nar_logFC)) 


for_plot <- matthaei_degs %>% 
  left_join(mirtrones_matthaei %>% select(gene_name, gene_id_mir), by=c('miRNA' = 'gene_id_mir'))  %>% 
  rename(in_gene = gene_name) %>% 
  left_join(logfc_our, by=c('in_gene' = 'gene')) %>% 
  left_join(logfc_nar, by=c('in_gene' = 'gene')) %>%  filter(!is.na(in_gene)) 



# check same genes
for_plot %>% group_by(in_gene) %>%    summarise(
    unique_mirna_count = n_distinct(miRNA))  %>%
  filter(unique_mirna_count > 1) #%>% DT::datatable(caption = "Mirtrone genes with multiple miRNA in them")



# check same mirnas
mir_dups <- for_plot %>% group_by(miRNA) %>%  summarise(
    unique_gene_count = n_distinct(in_gene))  %>%
  filter(unique_gene_count > 1) %>% pull(miRNA) #%T>% {
  #	DT::datatable(., caption = "MIR found in multiple genes") %>% print()}  %>% pull(miRNA) 

# MIR

# квантили fc повторяющихся мирнк
values <- c(-6.54, -4.33, 7.65)
sapply(values, function(x) mean(matthaei_degs$FoldChange <= x)) * 100


# rm and arange
for_plot <- for_plot %>% 
  filter( 
    !(miRNA %in% mir_dups),
    !(is.na(our_logFC) & is.na(nar_logFC))) %>% 
  arrange(logFC) %>% 
  rownames_to_column('fc_rank') %>% 
  mutate(fc_rank = as.numeric(fc_rank))
```

Сложность:

-   В миртронах matthaei есть много миРНК на 1 ген. По-хорошему тут нужно считать CPM на уровень транскриптов, но думаю будет ок если мы посчитаем их с равной экспр.

-   Также есть миРНК, сидящая в нескольких генах, ака местах генома, что с ней делать? -- Думаю просто выбросить, тк мы не сможем однозначно соопоставить её с несколькими генами.

## Matthaei DEGS {.tabset}

### mat mirtrones

Из-за повторяющихся хостов для нескольких миРНК факторные ранги неверные, нужно сделать численно.

```{r, fig.width=17, fig.height=8, echo=F}
# get orders

first_positive_gene <- for_plot %>%  arrange(logFC) %>% filter(logFC >= 0) %>%  slice(1) %>%  pull(fc_rank) - 0.5

for_plot_long <- for_plot %>%
  select(miRNA, in_gene, our_logFC, logFC, nar_logFC, fc_rank) %>%
  pivot_longer(
    cols = c(our_logFC, logFC, nar_logFC),
    names_to = "dataset",
    values_to = "logFC") %>%
  mutate(dataset = factor(dataset,
                          levels = c("our_logFC", "logFC", "nar_logFC"),
                          labels = c("Our Data", "Mat Data", "Nar Data"))) %>% 
  filter(dataset %in% c('Nar Data','Our Data'))
#%>% 
 # mutate(in_gene = factor(in_gene, levels = ordered_levels))

ggplot(for_plot_long , aes(x = fc_rank, y = logFC, fill = dataset)) +
 geom_col(position = position_dodge(width = 0.8), width = 0.7) + 
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 90, size = 9), axis.text.y = element_blank()) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = first_positive_gene, linetype = "dashed", color = "red") +
  scale_x_continuous(
    breaks = for_plot_long$fc_rank,
    labels = for_plot_long$in_gene,
    expand = c(0.02, 0.02) )+
  labs(title = 'matthaei degs with mirtrones, ordered by logFC', x= "Mirtrone host genes ordered by fold change", y="Log2FC")  + 
scale_fill_manual(
    name = "Dataset", values = c("Our Data" = "#1f77b4", "Nar Data" = "#ff7f0e"), 
    labels = c("Our Data" = "Nikitina et al.", "Nar Data" = "Chu et al.") )
```

пресекающиеся ДЭГи у нас и у Matth

```{r}


for_plot_long <- for_plot %>% filter(miRNA %in% diffexpressed_mirna$miRNA) %>% 
  arrange(logFC) %>% 
  rownames_to_column('fc_rank2') %>% 
  mutate(fc_rank2 = as.numeric(fc_rank2)) %>% 
  select(miRNA, in_gene, our_logFC, logFC, nar_logFC, fc_rank2) %>%
  
  pivot_longer(
    cols = c(our_logFC, logFC, nar_logFC),
    names_to = "dataset",
    values_to = "logFC") %>%
  mutate(dataset = factor(dataset,
                          levels = c("our_logFC", "logFC", "nar_logFC"),
                          labels = c("Our Data", "Mat Data", "Nar Data"))) %>% 
  filter(dataset %in% c('Nar Data','Our Data'))

first_positive_gene <- 6.5

ggplot(for_plot_long, aes(x = fc_rank2, y = logFC, fill = dataset)) +
 geom_col(position = position_dodge(width = 0.8), width = 0.7) + 
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 90, size = 15)) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = first_positive_gene, linetype = "dashed", color = "red") +
  scale_x_continuous(
    breaks = for_plot_long$fc_rank2,
    labels = for_plot_long$in_gene,
    expand = c(0.02, 0.02) )+
  labs(title = 'intersected matthaei degs with mirtrones, ordered by logFC') +
  scale_fill_manual(
    name = "Dataset", values = c("Our Data" = "#1f77b4", "Nar Data" = "#ff7f0e"), 
    labels = c("Our Data" = "Nikitina et al.", "Nar Data" = "Chu et al.") )
```

проверим по спм тоже самое, вдруг на наих логФС попадаются обравки генов, мелкие кусочки, нужно понять порядок спм каунтов, тк лфс нам этого не скажет

```{r, fig.width=13, fig.height=7, echo=F}
means_our <- cpm.symbol %>%
  rownames_to_column("gene") %>%
  mutate(our_mean_fecd = rowMeans(select(., starts_with("Dfu_"))),
    our_mean_control = rowMeans(select(., starts_with("C_")))) %>%
  select(gene, our_mean_fecd, our_mean_control)

means_nar <- nar %>%
  rownames_to_column("gene") %>%
  mutate(nar_mean_fecd = rowMeans(select(., starts_with("FECD"))),
    nar_mean_control = rowMeans(select(., starts_with("Ctrl")))) %>%
  select(gene, nar_mean_fecd, nar_mean_control)

for_plot <- matthaei_degs %>% 
  left_join(mirtrones_matthaei %>% select(gene_name, gene_id_mir), by=c('miRNA' = 'gene_id_mir'))  %>% 
  rename(in_gene = gene_name) %>% 
  left_join(means_our, by=c('in_gene' = 'gene')) %>% 
  left_join(means_nar, by=c('in_gene' = 'gene')) %>%  
  na.omit() %>% arrange(logFC) %>% 
  rownames_to_column('fc_rank') %>% 
  mutate(fc_rank = as.numeric(fc_rank))

first_positive_gene <- for_plot %>%  arrange(logFC) %>% filter(logFC >= 0) %>%  slice(1) %>%  pull(fc_rank) - 0.5


for_plot_long <- for_plot %>%
  select(miRNA, in_gene, logFC, nar_mean_control, nar_mean_fecd, our_mean_fecd, our_mean_control, fc_rank) %>%
  pivot_longer(
    cols = c(nar_mean_control, nar_mean_fecd, our_mean_fecd, our_mean_control),
    names_to = "dataset",
    values_to = "mean_cpm") %>%
  mutate(dataset = factor(dataset,
                          levels = c('nar_mean_control', 'nar_mean_fecd', 'our_mean_fecd', 'our_mean_control'),
                          labels = c('nar_mean_control', 'nar_mean_fecd', 'our_mean_fecd', 'our_mean_control'))) 
#%>% 
 # mutate(in_gene = factor(in_gene, levels = ordered_levels))

ggplot(for_plot_long, aes(x = fc_rank, y = mean_cpm, fill = dataset)) +
 geom_col(position = position_dodge(width = 0.8), width = 0.7) + 
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 90, size = 10)) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = first_positive_gene, linetype = "dashed", color = "red") +
  scale_x_continuous(
    breaks = for_plot_long$fc_rank,
    labels = for_plot_long$in_gene,
    expand = c(0.02, 0.02) )+
  labs(title = "matthaei degs' CPMs with mirtrones, ordered by logFC") #+ theme_minimal()
```

### matt hg genes

По HG, пересекающимся с matthe,обяз добавить перевал через 0 или подписать тупо.

matthaei degs with Host genes, ordered by logFC

```{r,  echo=F}
# rm hg
logfc_our$gene <- str_remove(logfc_our$gene, "HG")
logfc_nar$gene <- str_remove(logfc_nar$gene, "HG")

for_plot <- matthaei_degs %>% 
  left_join(logfc_our, by=c('HGNC_name' = 'gene')) %>%  
  left_join(logfc_nar, by=c('HGNC_name' = 'gene'))  %>% 
  na.omit() %>% arrange(logFC) %>% 
  rownames_to_column('fc_rank') %>% 
  mutate(fc_rank = as.numeric(fc_rank))



first_positive_gene <- for_plot %>%  arrange(logFC) %>% filter(logFC >= 0) %>%  slice(1) %>%  pull(fc_rank) - 0.5

for_plot_long <- for_plot %>%
```
